evals.name: new-cli-commands-missing-from-readme
evals.prompt: I just added two new CLI commands (rollback and logs) plus new deploy options (--timeout and --rollback-on-failure) and two new env vars (MYAPP_WEBHOOK_URL and MYAPP_MAX_RETRIES). The README only documents the old deploy and status commands. Sync the docs. The diff is at skills/sync-docs/evals/files/eval1.diff, the current README is at skills/sync-docs/evals/files/eval1_readme.md, the CLI entry point is at skills/sync-docs/evals/files/eval1_cli.ts, and the deploy handler is at skills/sync-docs/evals/files/eval1_deploy.ts.
evals.expected_output: Updated README with rollback and logs command sections, new deploy options documented, new env vars added to configuration table, architecture section left intact
expectations: Announces 'Syncing documentation with code changes' at the start
Uses git diff or reads the provided diff to identify what changed
Adds a section for the new 'rollback' command with its options (--version, --force) to the README
Adds a section for the new 'logs' command with its options (--follow, --since) to the README
Documents the new --timeout and --rollback-on-failure options under the deploy command
Adds MYAPP_WEBHOOK_URL and MYAPP_MAX_RETRIES to the environment variables table
Uses Edit tool rather than Write tool to update the existing README
Outputs a summary listing which files were updated and what changed
anti_expectations: Does not delete or rewrite the existing deploy/status command documentation
Does not touch the Architecture section since it was not affected by the changes
Does not create new documentation files when the README update is sufficient
Does not add boilerplate or filler content beyond what the code changes require

evals.name: stale-claude-md-after-api-v2-migration
evals.prompt: We migrated our FastAPI app from v1 to v2 API routes, added a new webhooks module, added middleware (rate limiting and correlation IDs), added new env vars (RATE_LIMIT_RPM, WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY, CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS), and bumped to version 2.0.0. The CLAUDE.md is outdated — it doesn't mention webhooks, middleware, or the new env vars, and it still references the old architecture. Sync the docs. The diff is at skills/sync-docs/evals/files/eval2.diff, the CLAUDE.md is at skills/sync-docs/evals/files/eval2_claude_md.md, the main app is at skills/sync-docs/evals/files/eval2_main.py, and the new webhooks router is at skills/sync-docs/evals/files/eval2_webhooks.py.
evals.expected_output: Updated CLAUDE.md with new env vars, webhooks module reference in architecture, middleware mention, corrected API version references
expectations: Announces 'Syncing documentation with code changes' at the start
Uses Glob and/or Grep to scan existing documentation for references to changed code
Adds RATE_LIMIT_RPM, WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY, and CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS to the environment variables table
Updates the Architecture section to mention the webhooks module (src/api/webhooks.py)
Documents the new middleware layer (rate limiting, correlation IDs) in the CLAUDE.md
Uses Edit tool to update the existing CLAUDE.md rather than rewriting with Write
Structures updated content for dual audience — uses explicit file paths and consistent headings
Outputs a summary listing which docs were updated and what changed
anti_expectations: Does not document internal implementation details of the webhook service or middleware
Does not add redundant comments that merely restate what the code does
Does not create separate new documentation files when updating CLAUDE.md suffices
Does not remove or rewrite existing sections that are still accurate (e.g., Development commands, Key Patterns)

evals.name: component-library-new-exports-stale-readme
evals.prompt: We added three new components to our React UI library (Tooltip, Toast, Select), added a 'danger' variant and 'xs' size to Button, added loading/tooltip/icon props to Button, added a 'system' theme option, and exported everything from the index. The README only documents Button, TextInput, and Modal with the old props. Sync the docs. The diff is at skills/sync-docs/evals/files/eval3.diff, the README is at skills/sync-docs/evals/files/eval3_readme.md, the updated Button is at skills/sync-docs/evals/files/eval3_button.tsx, and the index is at skills/sync-docs/evals/files/eval3_index.ts.
evals.expected_output: Updated README with new Button props documented, new components mentioned, theme option updated to include system, no unnecessary new files created
expectations: Announces 'Syncing documentation with code changes' at the start
Categorizes the changes — identifies new public APIs (Tooltip, Toast, Select), modified APIs (Button props/variants), and configuration changes (new theme option)
Updates the Button props list to include the new 'danger' variant, 'xs' size, 'loading', 'tooltip', and 'icon' props
Adds documentation sections for the new Tooltip, Toast, and Select components
Updates the Theming section to include the 'system' theme option
Checks existing docs for references to the old Button API that would be contradicted by the changes
Uses Edit tool to update the existing README rather than rewriting with Write
anti_expectations: Does not document private/internal implementation details like CSS module classes or clsx usage
Does not touch the TextInput or Modal sections since they were not changed
Does not create separate per-component documentation files when updating the README suffices
Does not add promotional or filler language to the component descriptions
